tasks.description: Quick checkup — L0+L1 output with 10 dimensions traffic-light
tasks.input: Give me a quick health check of my OpenClaw setup. Just the essentials — is everything OK or not?

scoring.5: Runs collection scripts; parses JSON output correctly
scoring.3: Runs at least some collection scripts
scoring.1: Manual data gathering instead of scripts

scoring.5: L0 one-line status (N✅ M⚠️ K❌) + L1 dimension grid (2 rows × 5); concise (<10 lines)
scoring.3: Status shown but not in L0/L1 format or missing dimensions
scoring.1: Verbose output for a quick check

scoring.5: Statuses reflect actual collected data; no fabricated information

tasks.input: What skills do I have installed? Are there any important ones I should add?

scoring.5: Runs collect-skills.sh; lists skills with versions and categories from JSON output
scoring.3: Lists skills but not from script output

scoring.5: Identifies missing essential skills from best-practices.md; explains why they're recommended
scoring.1: Generic suggestions unrelated to user's setup

scoring.5: Checks for broken dependencies and missing files per skill; reports ✅/⚠️/❌ status

tasks.description: Log analysis — dimension 4 with merged anomaly detection
tasks.input: Check my OpenClaw logs. Any errors or patterns I should worry about?

scoring.5: Runs collect-logs.sh; reports error count, error rate, and anomalies (spikes, OOM, stack traces) from merged output
scoring.3: Reads logs but misses anomaly detection

scoring.5: Identifies error patterns, rates; detects anomalies; determines ✅/⚠️/❌ status; references error-patterns.md and anomaly-patterns.md
scoring.3: Lists errors but no anomaly detection

scoring.5: Actionable fixes for detected patterns with commands; references PB-009 for anomaly response

tasks.input: Analyze my OpenClaw configuration. I have 25 skills installed and run about 50 sessions/day with moderate complexity. Is my config optimized?

scoring.5: Runs collect-config.sh; checks both config validity (dim 3) and agent parameters (dim 8); cross-validates
Specific concurrency/timeout recommendations justified by workload (25 skills, 50 sessions/day); references best-practices.md thresholds; reports ✅/⚠️/❌ per parameter
Each recommendation has a ready-to-execute command
Shows current value → recommended value with expected status change (⚠️→✅)

tasks.description: Performance bottleneck diagnosis via full 10-dimension checkup
tasks.input: OpenClaw feels sluggish. Sessions timeout frequently. Can you diagnose the performance issues?

scoring.5: Runs all 8 collection scripts; produces 10-dimension analysis; saves checkup data
Identifies specific bottleneck (memory/CPU/concurrency/gateway) from ❌/⚠️ dimensions; correlates across dimensions
scoring.3: Points to likely cause but weak evidence

scoring.5: Step-by-step fix plan with commands; references fix-playbooks.md; targets ❌ dimensions first
Specifies re-run checkup to verify (--fix flow); compare before/after status per dimension

tasks.input: My OpenClaw Gateway seems to be having issues. Some API calls fail intermittently. Can you check the gateway health?

scoring.5: Runs collect-health.sh; checks /, /openclaw, /hooks; reports status codes and latency; determines ✅/⚠️/❌
Correctly interprets statuses (e.g., root OK but /openclaw 503 = started but not operational); explains reachable vs operational
Specific recovery commands from PB-003; checks for port conflicts; suggests restart sequence; offers --fix

tasks.input: Check if all my OpenClaw tools and channels are properly set up. Are there any missing tools or misconfigured channels?

scoring.5: Runs collect-tools.sh; checks core CLI tools (node, curl, clawhub/openclaw) + core MCP tools; reports ✅/⚠️/❌; lists available vs missing
scoring.3: Checks some tools but misses MCP or CLI category

scoring.5: Runs collect-channels.sh; checks channel config validity, enabled status, webhook configuration; reports ✅/⚠️/❌
For missing tools: install commands; for misconfigured channels: setup instructions referencing setup.md

tasks.description: Multi-issue cascading failure — full checkup + smart fix
tasks.input: OpenClaw is in bad shape: random crashes, skills failing, strange log errors. I recently upgraded Node.js and installed several new skills. Help me untangle this.

scoring.5: Runs all 8 scripts; produces 10-dimension analysis; identifies multiple ❌/⚠️ dimensions; saves checkup
Identifies cascading cause chain across dimensions (e.g., version ❌ → skills ❌ → logs ❌); references error-patterns.md cascade patterns
Ordered fix plan: ❌ first then ⚠️; each step has command + rollback; uses fix-playbooks.md; offers to execute via --fix flow
scoring.3: Basic fix plan with some ordering

scoring.5: Re-runs affected scripts to verify (⚠️/❌→✅); recommends scheduled checkups; saves before/after checkup for comparison

tasks.input: Perform a comprehensive security audit of my OpenClaw installation. Scan for credential exposure, check file permissions, audit dependencies for CVEs, verify network security. Give me a full remediation plan.

scoring.5: Runs collect-security.sh; gets credential scan, file permissions, dependency audit, network exposure, VCS check; all sub-dimensions reported
Identifies specific issues; references security-checks.md patterns; credentials REDACTED; flags permissions, CVEs, network exposure
Priority-ordered remediation using PB-008; each fix has verification step; credential rotation; .gitignore fixes; permission corrections
All credential values shown as ***REDACTED***; only type + location disclosed

tasks.description: Migration planning with checkup baseline and comparison
tasks.input: I'm upgrading from OpenClaw 0.5.0 to 1.0.0. I have 20 skills and custom configs. Create a migration plan with risk assessment, using a full health baseline first. Save a checkup for future comparison.

scoring.5: Runs full 10-dimension checkup; saves to data/checkups/; generates L0+L1+L2 report; creates baseline for post-migration comparison
Step-by-step: backup → pre-checks → upgrade → post-checkup → compare before/after via snapshot-manager.sh compare
Identifies risks per dimension; estimates which dimensions may go from ✅→⚠️/❌; mitigation per risk
Complete rollback plan with checkpoints; post-migration checkup for comparison; uses --compare to show dimension status changes
